title: penstemon crandallii subsp. typicus
intro: Penstemon crandallii subsp. typicus, also known as the Crandall's beardtongue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Penstemon genus. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which contributes to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to leverage its health benefits and promote overall well-being.
